This demonstrates SpaceX's effort to maintain transparent communication channels with its investor base and keep stakeholders informed on company developments—a standard but important operational practice for private companies managing investor relations and capital access.

parent.name: X Freeze
parent.embed_html:
Space radiators are not sci-fi. The ISS already uses this physics. SpaceX is just scaling it with Starship

People act like cooling a data center in space is some impossible sci-fi problem. It is not. The physics is simple:

On Earth, heat can leave through air, water, fans,… pic.twitter.com/kGH1cN0PqX

— X Freeze (@XFreeze) June 15, 2026

ai_summary: The thread discusses SpaceX's technical approach to solving thermal management challenges for space-based data centers using radiator technology already proven on the ISS. Elon's reply establishes SpaceX's operational expertise and credibility in this domain, noting they operate over 10,000 satellites in orbit—more than all competitors combined—giving them practical knowledge to scale such systems. This illustrates how SpaceX's existing infrastructure and experience positions them to tackle emerging infrastructure challenges in space, relevant to their business strategy around space-based computing and Starship capabilities.
